hook_text: 5 reasons Omani companies delay HR software — and why each one costs more than the software.
body:
We've had the same conversation dozens of times.

Here's what we hear — and what the math actually says:

1. "We're too small to need it" (30-50 employees)
Reality: WPS compliance requirements don't scale with size. One MOL fine (OMR 150-500) often exceeds 3 months of software cost.

2. "Our process is working fine"
Reality: 9 of 12 companies we've audited had end-of-service calculation errors. "Working fine" often means "uncaught errors."

3. "We'll implement it after our busy season"
Reality: The busy season is when compliance errors happen. Implementation before the rush = protection during it.

4. "Our team will take time to learn new software"
Reality: Average SmartPRO Hub onboarding: 3 days. Average time spent fixing a WPS rejection manually: 4-6 hours. The math inverts quickly.

5. "We're waiting to see if the software proves itself in the market"
Reality: Every month of delay is another month of exposure. The software proves itself in your environment in 14 days.

The cost of waiting isn't zero. It's compounding.

---

Which of these have you told yourself? (No judgment — I've heard them all)
cta: DM "READY" if the math has finally shifted for you.
rejected_reason: pre-PR#12 backlog purge — queue hygiene 2026-07-15 (regenerate via corrected pipeline)
